stories.headline: US and Israel launch joint strikes on Iran, killing Supreme Leader Khamenei and sparking regional retaliation

articles.title: One killed and 11 injured at Dubai and Abu Dhabi airports as Iran strikes region
articles.description: Falling debris from intercepted Iranian drones and missiles cause widespread damage across the Gulf.

articles.title: Trump's bet on Iranian regime change could be his biggest gamble yet
articles.description: The US president may be forced to wage a political battle at home while presiding over a new war in the Middle East.

articles.title: Lyse Doucet: This is an extraordinary moment Iran has been preparing for
articles.description: The killing of the supreme leader will be a huge jolt to the Islamic Republic, which will try hard to show it has a plan in place.

articles.title: How Ayatollah Khamenei kept an iron grip on power for almost 40 years
articles.description: Iranian state TV confirms the supreme leader has been killed on the first day of massive US and Israeli air strikes on the country.

articles.title: What we know so far about the US-Israeli attacks and Iran's retaliation
articles.description: Iran responds by launching missiles and drones towards Israel and four Gulf Arab countries that host US military bases.

articles.title: UK troops and civilians being put at risk by Iran strikes, Healey says
articles.description: British military personnel at a base in Bahrain were "within several hundred yards" of a strike, the defence secretary says.

articles.title: Flights in and out of Middle East cancelled and diverted
articles.description: BA and Virgin Atlantic are among major airlines to ground services to the Middle East in light of the attacks.

articles.title: U.S.-Israeli strikes in Iran continue into 2nd day, as the region faces turmoil
articles.description: Israel said on Sunday it had launched more attacks on Iran, while the Iranian government continued strikes on Israel and on U.S. targets in Gulf states, Iraq and Jordan.

articles.title: Trump warns Iran not to retaliate after Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is killed
articles.description: The Iranian government has announced 40 days of mourning. The country's supreme leader was killed following an attack launched by the U.S. and Israel on Saturday against Iran.

articles.title: Explosion rocks Iran's capital as Israel says it is targeting the city
articles.description: Iran fired missiles at targets in Israel and Gulf Arab states Sunday after vowing massive retaliation for the killing of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei by the United States and Israel.

articles.title: Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is killed in Israeli strike, ending 36-year iron rule
articles.description: Khamenei, the Islamic Republic's second supreme leader, has been killed. He had held power since 1989, guiding Iran through difficult times — and overseeing the violent suppression of dissent.

articles.title: Here's how world leaders are reacting to the US-Israel strikes on Iran
articles.description: Several leaders voiced support for the operation – but most, including those who stopped short of condemning it, called for restraint moving forward.

articles.title: How could the U.S. strikes in Iran affect the world's oil supply?
articles.description: Despite sanctions, Iran is one of the world's major oil producers, with much of its crude exported to China.

articles.title: What to know about the U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran
articles.description: Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in Israeli airstrikes with U.S. support on Saturday. "Operation Epic Fury" will be "massive and ongoing," President Trump said.

articles.title: Iran strikes were launched without approval from Congress, deeply dividing lawmakers
articles.description: Top lawmakers were notified about the operation shortly before it was launched, but the White House did not seek authorization from Congress to carry out the strikes.

articles.title: Iran live updates: Trump warns Iran 'BETTER NOT' retaliate after vow from leader
articles.description: The U.S. and Israel launched a joint attack on Iran on Saturday, officials said.

articles.title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran's supreme leader, is dead, Trump says
articles.description: Khamenei, 86, had led Iran since the death in 1989 of the country's founding supreme leader.

articles.title: WATCH:  What strikes on Iran mean for the Middle East
articles.description: Former U.S. ambassador to NATO retired Lt. Gen. Doug Lute discusses the possible fallout from the joint U.S.-Israel attack.

articles.title: WATCH:  At least 201 people had been killed across Iran: Iranian Red Crescent Society
articles.description: Michael Singh, the managing director and senior fellow at the Washington Institute, joins to examine the U.S. military strikes in the Middle East.

articles.title: WATCH:  Missiles land in Dubai
articles.description: ABC News’ Lama Hasan reports from the region on the Iranian response to U.S. attacks, with the UAE saying a civilian was killed by falling debris.

articles.title: WATCH:  Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman says president, supreme leader 'safe and sound'
articles.description: Esmail Baghaei joins ABC News following US and Israeli strikes on Iran early Saturday morning

articles.title: Iran holds funeral for Khamenei as war with U.S. and Israel continues
articles.description: Iran's regime was hobbled by a day of war with the U.S. and Israel, but as people gathered for the ayatollah's funeral the future remained uncertain, and missiles kept flying.

articles.title: 2/28: CBS Weekend News
articles.description: Trump says Iran’s supreme leader killed in joint strikes by U.S., Israel; What we know after U.S., Israel attacks in Iran

articles.title: World leaders and governments react to U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran
articles.description: U.S. allies and adversaries responded to the joint attacks by the U.S. and Israel on Iran with concern over the risks of a new war in the Middle East.

articles.title: Live Updates: U.S.-Israel launch another round of strikes on Iran
articles.description: President Trump said that "heavy and pinpoint bombing" of Iran would "continue, uninterrupted throughout the week or, as long as necessary."

articles.title: War with Iran | CBS News Special
articles.description: Tony Dokoupil anchors special coverage of the large-scale military operation launched by the U.S. and Israel on Iran, killing Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who ruled for nearly 40 years. President Trump said the U.S. would continue "heavy and pinpoint bombing" throughout the week or "as long as necessary."

articles.title: "I have been singing, screaming, celebrating with my people," Iranian-American journalist says
articles.description: Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in strikes by the U.S. and Israel on Saturday. CBS News contributor Masih Alinejad, who has survived three plots by Iran's regime to kill or kidnap her, discusses the crippling of the Iranian regime.

articles.title: Potential impact of Iran strikes on oil, gas prices
articles.description: Some Americans are concerned about how attacks on Iran could impact the economy. CBS News' Jericka Duncan discusses the potential impact.

articles.title: "Unprecedented cooperation" between all levels of government to deter attacks in U.S., expert says
articles.description: CBS News national security contributor Sam Vinograd, who served as assistant secretary for counterterrorism for the Department of Homeland Security, discusses how the U.S. is working to share intelligence and counter any domestic attacks.

articles.title: Destabilization of Iran will have "huge implications way beyond the Middle East," analyst says
articles.description: With the U.S. and Israel launching an armed conflict with Iran, Saturday could mark one of the most consequential days for the Middle East in generations. Analyst and author Douglas Murray and CBS News' Major Garrett break it all down.

articles.title: Rep. Rick Crawford says "the timing was perfect" for attacks in Iran
articles.description: Rep. Rick Crawford of Arkansas, an Army veteran and chair of the House Intelligence Committee, joins "CBS Evening News" to discuss why the U.S. and Israel launched attacks on Iran at this time, ahead of planned nuclear talks.

articles.title: Trump tells CBS News attacks could bring a diplomatic solution closer
articles.description: Chief Washington analyst Robert Costa spoke with President Trump by phone on Saturday. Mr. Trump told Costa he was somewhat surprised at Iran's retaliatory response.

articles.title: Retired rear admiral estimates at least 2 weeks of conflict with Iran
articles.description: Retired Rear Admiral Mark Montgomery and Marine Corp. veteran Elliot Ackerman join "CBS Evening News" to discuss the U.S. and Israeli operations in Iran.

articles.title: The C.I.A. Helped Pinpoint a Gathering of Iranian Leaders. Then Israel Struck.
articles.description: The killing of Iran’s supreme leader and other top Iranian officials came after close intelligence sharing between the United States and Israel, according to people familiar with the operation.

articles.title: Why Diplomacy Was Doomed: Trump’s Issue Was Iran’s Leadership Itself
articles.description: President Trump’s envoys negotiated with Iran over its nuclear program. But Mr. Trump and Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel wanted much more.

articles.title: See a Breakdown of Ayatollah Khamenei and Other Top Iranian Leaders
articles.description: Among those killed in strikes, according to the U.S. and Israel, were Iran’s supreme leader and three of his top military commanders.

articles.title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Hard-Line Cleric Who Made Iran a Regional Power, Is Dead at 86
articles.description: As Iran’s second supreme leader, he brutally crushed dissent at home and expanded Iran’s footprint abroad, challenging Saudi Arabia for regional dominance.

articles.title: A Tyrant Falls. Dangerous Uncertainty Begins.
articles.description: No one should mourn Ayatollah Khamenei, but Trump is creating the opportunity for chaos and violence with his campaign against Iran.

articles.title: War and Peace Cannot Be Left to One Man — Especially Not This Man
articles.description: The Constitution isn’t a technicality.

articles.title: Trump says 'a large amount' of Iran's leadership is gone
articles.description: President Donald Trump said Saturday that U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran killed Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and other top leaders.

articles.title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, religious cleric who ruled Iran for decades, killed in strikes
articles.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who reigned as Iran’s supreme leader for the past 36 years, was killed in a sweeping U.S. and Israeli attack on the country Saturday.

articles.title: Trump on reports of Ayatollah's death: 'We feel that is a correct story'
articles.description: In a conversation with Meet the Press moderator Kristen Welker, President Trump said he believed reports on the death of Iran's supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in strikes against the country are "a correct story."

articles.title: Iran's foreign minister says he's unsure why U.S. attacked during nuclear talks

articles.title: Details on weapons used in Iran strike
articles.description: NBC News’ Courtney Kube reports on the ‘suicide’ drones used in the U.S. attack on Iran.

articles.title: Inside President Trump’s decision to strike
articles.description: As recently as Friday President Trump was talking up efforts to find a negotiated solution to the U.S. tensions with Iran. NBC News’ Garrett Haake reports from the White House on the details that led to the unprecedented strike.

articles.title: Gas prices likely to rise after attack in Iran
articles.description: Analysts tell NBC News’ Allie Canal that oil prices could jump as much as $20 a barrel driving gas prices up if there is widespread disruption in the Middle East.

articles.title: Iran's foreign minister says he's unsure why U.S. attacked during nuclear talks

articles.title: Trump announces beginning of 'major combat operations in Iran'
articles.description: President Donald Trump confirmed the strikes on Iran in a video message shared on Truth Social, reiterating that "they can never have a nuclear weapon."

articles.title: Democrats and a few Republicans say they'll force a vote to limit Trump in Iran

articles.title: Thousands gather in Tehran to mourn Khamenei's death
articles.description: Thousands gather in Tehran to mourn Khamenei's death

articles.title: See where U.S., Israeli strikes have hit Iran and where Iran has retaliated

articles.title: Outside White House, hundreds protest attack on Iran, urge end to conflict

articles.title: US Issues Worldwide Travel Warning After Iran Strikes

articles.title: Iran Disrupts Vital Oil Route. Price Rises Coming

articles.title: Man accuses Israel of war crimes as he holds remains of girl killed in Iran
articles.description: An Iranian man accused Israel of war crimes as he cradled remains of a girl killed in an attack on an elementary school.

articles.title: ‘US and Israel’s war on Iran is based on a flawed strategy’

articles.title: Israel closes Gaza’s Rafah crossing amid attacks on Iran
articles.description: The crossing with Egypt is considered vital for delivery of humanitarian aid and evacuation of critically ill patients.

articles.title: Lawmakers, world leaders react to Khamenei’s death after Iran strikes
articles.description: The death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on Saturday following joint U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran was met with praise from some lawmakers and world leaders, and left others condemning the Trump administration and declaring its actions illegal. Rep. Yassamin Ansari (D-Ariz.), the only Iranian American member of Congress, called Khamenei the “epitome of evil” and said,…

articles.description: Iran now stands without its supreme leader for 36 years, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, after joint strikes from the U.S. and Israel on Saturday killed him and attacked military command posts and missile sites across the country. President Trump announced the strikes at 2:30 a.m. EST in a video shared on Truth Social. The mission, dubbed…

articles.title: Hegseth calls Iran strikes ‘most lethal’ aerial operation in history
articles.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth late on Saturday broke his silence about the U.S. strikes on Iran, which he described as the “most lethal, most complex, and most-precision aerial operation in history.” “The Iranian regime had their chance, yet refused to make a deal — and now they are suffering the consequences,” Hegseth said in a…

articles.title: US, Iran representatives clash at UN Security Council meeting
articles.description: American and Iranian representatives had a terse exchange Saturday at an emergency meeting of the UN Security Council, hours after the U.S. and Israel attacked the Middle Eastern country with a barrage of missile strikes. “I advise to the representative of the United States to be polite,” Iranian Ambassador to the UN Amir Saeid Iravani…

articles.title: Iran attack becomes latest flashpoint in Senate races
articles.description: The U.S. strikes on Iran early Saturday has become the latest major issue addressed by candidates running in some of the more competitive Senate races. articles.title: Trump offering immunity to IRGC, Iranian military and police forces
articles.description: President Trump on Saturday said he will offer immunity to members of Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), along with its military and police forces, after the U.S. and Israel attacked the country earlier in the day. “We are hearing that many of their IRGC, Military, and other Security and Police Forces, no longer want…

articles.title: Trump says Iran supreme leader Khamenei is dead
articles.description: President Trump on Saturday afternoon said Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is dead following U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran that the White House intended to lead to regime change for Tehran. Trump said the longtime U.S. enemy was dead about an hour after multiple news outlets reported that U.S. and Israel believed he…

articles.title: Lawler rips Massie, Democrats over war powers push after US strikes in Iran
articles.description: Rep. Mike Lawler (R-N.Y.) on Saturday ripped into Rep. Thomas Massie (R-Ky.) and Democratic lawmakers pushing for a war powers resolution to rein in President Trump after the U.S. and Israel carried out joint strikes on Iran. Lawler said the president must notify Congress within 48 hours of deploying troops or carrying out an attack,…

articles.title: Democrats split over response to Trump’s Iran strikes
articles.description: The breaks between the party’s peace and interventionist wings are ones Democrats will have to contend with ahead of the midterms.

articles.title: Sustained war with Iran could drain US missile stockpiles, test escalation control

articles.description: Dozens of direct or indirect attacks on Americans both militarily and civilian-wise have been carried out or aided by Iran's Supreme Leaders Khomeini and Khamenei

articles.description: Iran postwar planning differs from America's Iraq experience and with the Marshall Plan precedent there can also be benefits to being involved.

articles.description: Iran enters a pivotal transition following Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s death, as clerical elites and the powerful IRGC maneuver to preserve the Islamic Republic.

articles.description: VOA Persian service reportedly continues broadcasting inside Iran despite regime communication restrictions, covering "Operation Epic Fury" and Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's death.

articles.description: Iran's internet connectivity flatlined at 1% after strikes on a Tehran regime compound, marking one of the world's rare wartime national blackouts according to global internet monitors.

articles.title: Mamdani's response to Trump's Iran strike sparks conservative backlash: 'Rooting for the ayatollah'
articles.description: NYC socialist Mayor Zohran Mamdani faces conservative backlash after condemning Trump's Iran strike that killed Ayatollah Khamenei, calling it an illegal war.

articles.title: Iranians celebrate worldwide after supreme leader is killed in Israeli strikes
articles.description: Coordinated strikes killed Iran's Khamenei, and citizens celebrated across the world, waving flags and chanting about freedom after decades of oppression.

articles.title: Iran vows 'decisive' self-defense at UN after Trump kills supreme leader in Operation Epic Fury
articles.description: Iran's U.N. ambassador condemned U.S. strikes that killed Supreme Leader Khamenei, calling the attack a sovereignty violation at a Security Council meeting.

articles.title: MIKE DAVIS: Why Trump's Iran strike was necessary and lawful
articles.description: Mike Davis discusses presidential war powers and constitutional authority for military action without congressional approval in various international contexts.

articles.title: LISA DAFTARI: Trump didn’t start a war; he ended one, and Iranians are celebrating
articles.description: After 47 years of suffocation, Iranian people seize their own destiny as celebrations stretch from Tehran to Los Angeles in an unprecedented moment of hope.

articles.title: Key military sites targeted inside Iran as part of coordinated US-Israeli strikes
articles.description: The U.S. and Israel targeted military and nuclear sites in airstrikes. Israeli strikes also killed more than 40 Iranian leaders, including Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

articles.title: CIA tracked Iran Supreme Leader Khamenei for months — intel helped US, Israel time strike during meeting: report
articles.description: US intelligence officials had tracked Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei for months, learning and understanding his movement until he met with top regime officials inside his fortified compound during the deadly strikes Saturday.

articles.title: Trump teases ‘good candidates’ to lead Iran after Khamenei’s death
articles.description: President Donald Trump revealed that he has a potential leader in mind for Iran after strikes killed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. Khamenei was killed on Saturday after Israel struck his compound, ending his 36-year reign and leaving the regime in disarray as it moves to appoint a successor while joint U.S. and Israeli strikes continue. […]

Security Council on Saturday. “Iran’s continued pursuit of advanced missile capabilities, coupled with its refusal to abandon nuclear ambitions despite diplomatic opportunities, presents a grave and mounting danger,” Waltz told the […]

articles.title: Shapiro blasts Trump’s decision to strike Iran but admits regime change needed
articles.description: Gov. Josh Shapiro (D-PA) seemingly wavered over President Donald Trump’s decision to strike Iran early Saturday, lamenting what he sees as lacking a “clear plan” for the future of its government while simultaneously expressing the need for new leadership. “In going to war with Iran, the President has not adequately explained why this war is […]

articles.title: Congress scrambles for answers after Trump Iran strike
articles.description: Congressional leaders are rushing to get lawmakers up to speed on a military conflict with Iran that is rekindling a fight over President Donald Trump‘s war powers. Republicans in the House and Senate are working with the Trump administration to set up all-member briefings on the overnight military operation that killed top Iranian leaders, including […]

articles.title: Khamenei aides, IRGC commander among those killed by Israeli strikes so far

Israel and the United States conducted strikes starting overnight, with the Israel Defense Forces reportedly targeting regime leaders and the U.S. military hitting Iran’s ballistic missile and nuclear programs. Retaliatory strikes from […]

articles.title: Trump says Khamenei is dead: ‘The single greatest chance for the Iranian people to take back their Country’
articles.description: President Donald Trump on Saturday afternoon declared that Iranian Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, a man he described as “one of the most evil people in history,” is dead, following joint U.S.-Israeli strikes against Tehran.  “This is not only Justice for the people of Iran, but for all Great Americans, and those people from many Countries throughout […]

articles.title: Monitoring the situation: White House releases photos of Trump, Rubio and team being briefed on Iran operation
articles.description: The White House has released images showing President Donald Trump and his team from his makeshift Situation Room as strikes were launched against the Islamic Republic of Iran. The images, shared by the White House’s social media team, capture meetings held by both Trump and Vice President JD Vance amid the beginnings of “Operation Epic […]

articles.title: Iran Chose the Hard Way
articles.description: We watch the awesome display of American military might with humility, and wish Trump and the U.S. military every success.

articles.title: The Time for Regime Change in Iran Has Arrived
articles.description: The Trump administration is right: Only a change of regime in Tehran will end the threat Iran poses to Americans and our interests.

articles.title: In Iran, Trump Reaches a Turning Point
articles.description: The U.S. strikes on Iran will be game-changing for the Middle East.

analysis.summary: The United States and Israel launched joint military strikes on Iran dubbed 'Operation Epic Fury,' killing Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and other top leaders. Iran retaliated with missiles and drones targeting Israel and US military bases in Gulf Arab states, causing casualties and damage in Dubai, Abu Dhabi, and elsewhere. The strikes, launched without Congressional authorization, triggered protests, debates over war powers, disrupted global travel, and raised fears of rising oil prices and a wider regional conflict.
agreements: The US and Israel launched joint strikes on Iran in an operation called 'Operation Epic Fury'
Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in the strikes, ending his approximately 36-year rule
Other top Iranian military and government leaders were also killed alongside Khamenei
Trump announced the strikes via a video on Truth Social around 2:30 AM EST on Saturday
Iran retaliated with missiles and drones targeting Israel and US military bases in Gulf Arab states
At least one civilian was killed by falling debris at Dubai/Abu Dhabi airports in the UAE
Iran declared 40 days of mourning for Khamenei
The strikes were launched without prior Congressional authorization
The UN Security Council held an emergency meeting where US and Iranian representatives clashed
Major airlines cancelled or diverted flights across the Middle East
Oil and gas prices were expected to rise significantly due to the conflict
Protests against the strikes erupted in multiple US cities including Washington DC and New York
Some Iranian diaspora communities celebrated Khamenei's death worldwide
Trump stated the bombing campaign would continue 'throughout the week or as long as necessary'
The strikes targeted military command posts, ballistic missile sites, drone launch sites, nuclear program facilities, and military airfields

disagreements.topic: Legality and constitutionality of strikes without Congressional approval

positions.position: Published opinion piece arguing the strikes were 'necessary and lawful' under presidential war powers authority
Reported Waltz defended strikes as 'lawful' at the UN; Rep. Lawler criticized those pushing war powers resolutions
Published editorial titled 'War and Peace Cannot Be Left to One Man' arguing the Constitution requires Congressional approval
Emphasized strikes were 'launched without approval from Congress, deeply dividing lawmakers'
Framed the issue as Democratic lawmakers condemning strikes and emphasizing 'lack of congressional approval'
Reported Democrats and some Republicans would 'force a vote to limit Trump in Iran'

disagreements.topic: Whether the strikes primarily benefit the US or Israel

positions.position: Framed it as 'Netanyahu's war,' with analysts saying strikes benefit Israel, not the US, and contradict Trump's own criticism of regime change
Framed the operation as benefiting US national security and the Iranian people, with opinion pieces calling it ending a war rather than starting one
Argued regime change serves American interests and the threat Iran poses to Americans justified the strikes
Reported Iran's foreign minister questioning why the US attacked during ongoing nuclear talks, implying Israel drove the decision

disagreements.topic: Overall framing of the strikes — justified action vs. reckless escalation

positions.position: Overwhelmingly positive framing: 'Trump didn't start a war; he ended one'; emphasized Iranian celebrations and Iran's decades of aggression
Supportive: 'Iran Chose the Hard Way'; called regime change necessary and wished Trump success
Cautious framing: called it Trump's 'biggest gamble yet' that could force him to wage political battle at home while presiding over a new war
Critical framing: 'A Tyrant Falls. Dangerous Uncertainty Begins' — acknowledged Khamenei's tyranny but warned Trump is 'creating the opportunity for chaos and violence'
Strongly critical: called the war 'based on a flawed strategy,' emphasized civilian casualties including a girl killed in a school, and highlighted protests

disagreements.topic: Emphasis on civilian casualties in Iran

positions.position: Prominently featured civilian casualties, including a man holding remains of a girl killed in an elementary school and accusations of Israeli war crimes
Reported at least 201 people killed across Iran according to the Iranian Red Crescent Society
Focused almost exclusively on military targets, Iranian celebrations, and the precision of the strikes, with minimal coverage of civilian casualties
Referenced the strikes as 'heavy and pinpoint bombing,' largely focused on military and strategic aspects

disagreements.topic: Whether there is a viable path forward after Khamenei's death

positions.position: Reported Trump teasing 'good candidates' to lead Iran, suggesting a US-backed leadership transition is possible
Published Mark Levin opinion arguing the US should be involved in post-war planning, citing Marshall Plan precedent
Reported this is an 'extraordinary moment' but emphasized Iran will 'try hard to show it has a plan in place,' suggesting uncertainty
Reported Iran forming an interim council with Ayatollah Arafi, the president, and chief justice to oversee transition — suggesting the regime has a succession plan
Warned of 'dangerous uncertainty' and implied the aftermath could lead to chaos similar to other regime change efforts
Gov. Shapiro criticized Trump for lacking a 'clear plan' for Iran's future while also admitting regime change was needed

disagreements.topic: Characterization of domestic US political reaction

positions.position: Focused on Democrats being internally split between peace and interventionist wings ahead of midterms
Reported it as a flashpoint in Senate races, with reactions across the political spectrum
Noted antiwar MAGA wing exists but emphasized Republicans largely rallied behind Trump with 'hawks heaping praise'
Highlighted conservative backlash against critics of the strike, framing opposition as 'rooting for the ayatollah'

disagreements.topic: Initial confusion over Khamenei's status

positions.position: Aired Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman claiming the president and supreme leader were 'safe and sound' shortly after the strikes
Reported Iranian state TV eventually confirmed the supreme leader was killed
Reported Trump initially said 'we feel that is a correct story' when asked about Khamenei's death, indicating it was not immediately confirmed

uniqueAngles.angle: Reported that UK troops at a base in Bahrain were 'within several hundred yards' of an Iranian retaliatory strike, with Defence Secretary Healey warning that British military and civilians were being put at risk

Covered on-the-ground protests outside the White House, detailing demonstrations that erupted across the country

stories.headline: Texas primary elections heat up with competitive Senate races in both parties

articles.title: The Texas Senate Primary Offers a Test Case for Each Party
articles.description: Should Democrats concentrate on swing voters or their base? Can more traditional Republicans win in the MAGA era? Tuesday’s Senate primary in Texas will show the direction the parties are taking.

articles.title: How Wesley Hunt of Texas Is Working in Plain Sight With Outside Groups
articles.description: Exchanges between two X accounts appear to offer a vivid example of how campaigns may sidestep campaign-finance law to share strategic information.

articles.title: Generational change becomes focal point in Texas, North Carolina Democratic battles
articles.description: Several Democratic primaries in Texas and North Carolina are teeing up generational battles in the party. Rep. Al Green (D-Texas), 78, is facing a challenge from newly-elected 37-year-old Rep. Christian Menefee (D-Texas) in Texas’s redrawn 18th Congressional District in the Houston area.  Rep. Valerie Foushee (D-N.C.), 69, is fending off a primary challenge from 32-year-old…

articles.title: State of Texas: Poll tracks U.S. Senate race, Trump approval in Texas
articles.description: Tuesday is one of the most consequential days of the year in Texas politics. Voters will decide who appears on the November ballot - or in many cases, who advances to a May runoff.

articles.title: Kamala Harris backs Jasmine Crockett in bitter Texas Democratic Senate primary
articles.description: The former vice president recorded a robocall to boost Crockett over James Talarico.

articles.title: Bannon blasts Trump campaign aides in Texas Senate showdown
articles.description: Steve Bannon has gone all-in for Ken Paxton in Texas. But two of Trump's top campaign hands are backing John Cornyn — opening up a rift on the right.

articles.title: Republicans are freaking out over Texas Senate race
articles.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton is the man to beat ahead of Tuesday’s primary — and could put Senate Republicans’ majority at risk

articles.title: ‘Sweat equity’: Wesley Hunt doesn’t shy away from missed votes ahead of Texas Senate primary
articles.description: SAN ANTONIO — Rep. Wesley Hunt (R-TX) is everywhere in Texas and showing he’s working, leaving his supporters unbothered by his lack of attendance in Washington.  Hunt has missed 69 out of the 158 votes in Congress since launching his uphill campaign to unseat longtime Sen. John Cornyn (R-TX). Yet, even as the lack of […]

analysis.summary: Texas primary elections on Tuesday feature competitive Senate races in both parties, with the Republican contest pitting Sen. John Cornyn against Attorney General Ken Paxton and Rep. Wesley Hunt in a battle over the party's MAGA direction, while Democrats face a choice between Rep. Jasmine Crockett and Rep. James Talarico amid debates over base mobilization versus swing-voter appeal. The races are seen as bellwethers for broader party dynamics heading into November.
agreements: Texas holds its primary elections on Tuesday, with consequential Senate races in both parties
The Republican Senate primary features a competitive multi-candidate field including John Cornyn and Ken Paxton
The Democratic Senate primary pits Jasmine Crockett against James Talarico
The Republican primary reflects internal tensions within the party over MAGA alignment
Wesley Hunt is a candidate in the Republican Senate primary
Many races may result in May runoffs rather than outright primary winners

Frames the race as a test of whether 'more traditional Republicans can win in the MAGA era,' without explicitly naming a frontrunner

Frames the Democratic contests primarily through the lens of generational change within the party

Detailed the specific rift between Steve Bannon (backing Paxton) and Trump's own campaign operatives (backing Cornyn), exposing a fracture in MAGA-aligned leadership

stories.headline: Epstein investigations, newly released files, and congressional hearings draw continued scrutiny

articles.title: Why the Epstein Investigations Took So Long and Did So Little
articles.description: Decades of tips and investigations yielded charges against only two people. A combination of missed chances, narrow laws and prosecutors’ limited focus helps explain why.

articles.title: The U.S. Released the Epstein Files. The Arrests Are Overseas.
articles.description: While the latest tranche of documents about the sex offender Jeffrey Epstein has exposed a deeply American scandal, it is Europe where the aggressive enforcement is taking place.

articles.title: How The Times Is Digging Into Millions of Pages of Epstein Files
articles.description: Two dozen journalists. A pile of pages that would reach the top of the Empire State Building. And an effort to find the next revelation in a sprawling case.

articles.title: Democrats warn GOP that Bill and Hillary Clinton testimony will boomerang on them
articles.description: House Republicans won a political victory this week in hauling former President Clinton and former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton before the Oversight and Government Reform Committee to testify in the case of Jeffrey Epstein. But Democrats are already warning that the extraordinary development will boomerang on the GOP whenever Democrats win back the House…

analysis.summary: Newly released Epstein files continue to generate scrutiny, with investigations revealing decades of missed chances by U.S. prosecutors while European authorities pursue more aggressive enforcement. Meanwhile, congressional hearings featuring testimony from Bill and Hillary Clinton before the House Oversight Committee have added a sharp partisan dimension to the ongoing case.
agreements: The Epstein case represents a sprawling, long-running scandal involving significant volumes of newly released documents
U.S. legal and investigative responses to Epstein's crimes have been widely seen as inadequate relative to the scale of the offenses
The case continues to generate major political and legal developments in 2026

disagreements.topic: Primary framing of accountability failures

positions.position: Frames the story primarily as a systemic failure of U.S. law enforcement and prosecutors, citing missed chances, narrow laws, and limited prosecutorial focus over decades
Frames the story primarily through a partisan political lens, focusing on congressional subpoenas of the Clintons and the strategic implications for both parties

disagreements.topic: Emphasis on where meaningful action is occurring

positions.position: Highlights that aggressive enforcement and arrests are taking place in Europe, particularly the UK, rather than in the United States
Focuses on U.S. congressional hearings as the key arena of action, with no mention of international enforcement efforts

uniqueAngles.angle: Provides a detailed behind-the-scenes account of its own investigative effort, involving two dozen journalists reviewing millions of pages of documents — a pile that would reach the top of the Empire State Building

Reports that Democrats are warning Republicans that subpoenaing the Clintons sets a precedent that will 'boomerang' on the GOP when Democrats regain the House, introducing a forward-looking partisan strategy element absent from other coverage

stories.headline: US military mistakenly shoots down Customs and Border Protection drone near Mexico border

articles.title: Democrats outraged at US military’s downing of CBP drone near Mexico border
articles.description:
Second time in two weeks military used laser to attack what it mistakenly thought was a threat, disrupting air traffic

Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox

Democratic members of Congress have expressed astonishment and anger at what they claim is the incompetence of the Trump administration after the US military used a laser on Thursday to shoot down what it thought was a threatening drone on the US-Mexico border in Texas but later turned out to belong

articles.title: WATCH:  FAA restricts airspace over El Paso after U.S. military shoots down border drone
articles.description: The U.S. military shot down a Customs and Border Protection drone it mistakenly believed was a danger using a high-powered laser.

articles.title: U.S. military used a laser to shoot down a Border Protection drone
articles.description: The incident, which prompted the closure of airspace near Fort Hancock, Texas, occurred weeks after the use of a counter-drone laser by DHS personnel grounded flights at the El Paso airport.

analysis.summary: The U.S. military mistakenly shot down a Customs and Border Protection drone near the Texas-Mexico border using a high-powered laser, believing it was a threat. The incident prompted airspace restrictions in the area and marked the second such laser-related incident in recent weeks, following a previous event that disrupted flights at El Paso airport.

stories.headline: Aftermath of Supreme Court ruling striking down Trump's reciprocal tariffs reshapes trade and politics

articles.title: China emerges as big winner from Supreme Court's tariff ruling
articles.description: HONG KONG — Global trade has been upended again after the Supreme Court struck down President Donald Trump’s “reciprocal” tariffs, with U.S. trading partners and businesses around the world grasping to understand the system that replaces them

articles.title: Trump tariff refund fight in motion as small businesses take helm
articles.description: The fight for tariff refunds following the Supreme Court’s decision to void President Trump’s sweeping levies is underway.  A Michigan auto parts store and New York wine importer are leading the charge, and they’re moving fast.  Hundreds of small businesses and major corporations alike have rushed to court to reclaim the unlawfully collected funds, as…

articles.title: Dems refuse to make 2024 mistakes in the wake of tariff ruling
articles.description: The party is betting that a strong economic message will resonate better than emphasizing that the president is violating the law with his trade agenda.

analysis.summary: Following the Supreme Court's ruling striking down President Trump's reciprocal tariffs, the aftermath is playing out across multiple fronts: internationally, with China emerging as a key beneficiary; legally, with businesses rushing to reclaim refunds on unlawfully collected tariffs; and politically, with Democrats crafting their messaging strategy around the ruling's economic implications.
agreements: The Supreme Court struck down President Trump's reciprocal tariffs, ruling them unlawful
The ruling has had sweeping consequences across trade, business, and politics
The decision has created significant disruption and uncertainty in the existing trade framework

disagreements.topic: Primary framing of the ruling's significance

Frames the ruling primarily as a domestic political story, focusing on how Democrats are strategizing around it for electoral advantage

disagreements.topic: Whether the key issue is lawfulness or economic impact

positions.position: Reports that Democrats are deliberately choosing to emphasize affordability and economic impact over the argument that Trump violated the law

Highlights that specific small businesses — a Michigan auto parts store and a New York wine importer — are leading the refund legal fight, and that both small businesses and major corporations have rushed to court to reclaim funds

Reports on Democrats' deliberate strategic choice to focus on an economic/affordability message rather than a rule-of-law message, explicitly framing it as learning from mistakes made in the 2024 election cycle
